changes_made: Renamed k8s_node_configuration_management_enabled to k8s_node_kubelet_configuration_managed for specificity
review_notes: The control CM-6-b is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves implementing configuration settings, which is a critical aspect of Kubernetes security. The function names mostly follow the naming conventions, but one function needed refinement for better specificity. The functions map well to Kubernetes security controls, ensuring that configurations are properly set and managed.
title: Configuration Settings - Implement the configuration settings;
step1_reasoning: The control CM-6-b focuses on implementing configuration settings, which in a Kubernetes context can be mapped to ensuring that various configurations are properly set and managed. This includes ensuring that pod security contexts are defined to enforce security settings, API server flags are configured securely, node configurations are managed (potentially through tools like kubelet configuration), and admission controllers are properly configured to enforce policies.
